module.name: Cybersecurity
module.description: Advanced training in cyber defense, ethical hacking, and information warfare.

cybersecurity_digital_forensics.name: Digital Forensics
cybersecurity_digital_forensics.description: Techniques for analyzing and recovering digital evidence from cyber incidents.

cybersecurity_history_of_cybersecurity.name: History of Cybersecurity
cybersecurity_history_of_cybersecurity.description: Tracing the evolution of cybersecurity from the early days of networking to modern web security policies, including CORS and decentralized security concerns.

cybersecurity_malware_analysis.name: Malware Analysis
cybersecurity_malware_analysis.description: Dissecting malicious software to understand its behavior and develop countermeasures.

cybersecurity_network_security.name: Network Security
cybersecurity_network_security.description: Securing computer networks against cyber threats and unauthorized access.

cybersecurity_penetration_testing.name: Penetration Testing
cybersecurity_penetration_testing.description: Training in ethical hacking techniques to identify and exploit vulnerabilities in systems.

cybersecurity_social_engineering_defense.name: Social Engineering Defense
cybersecurity_social_engineering_defense.description: Preventing human-targeted attacks such as phishing, deception, and psychological exploitation.

cybersecurity_threat_intelligence.name: Threat Intelligence
cybersecurity_threat_intelligence.description: Gathering and analyzing intelligence on cyber threats and adversary tactics.

cybersecurity_digital_forensics_forensic.name: Extraction and Escape Tactics
cybersecurity_digital_forensics_forensic.description: Advanced techniques for escaping hostile zones and extracting high-value personnel.

cybersecurity_history_of_cybersecurity_h.description: Understanding the origins of cross-origin security policies, the technical decisions that led to their adoption, and their impact on modern web applications.

cards.title: The Birth of the Same-Origin Policy (SOP) in 1995
cards.description: The Same-Origin Policy (SOP) was first introduced by Netscape in 1995 as a security mechanism to prevent malicious websites from accessing data on different domains within the same browser session. This card covers key aspects including Netscape Navigator's introduction of JavaScript in 1995 led to security concerns., Without SOP, any website could read data from any other website open in the browser., SOP ensured that web pages could only access resources (such as cookies and DOM elements) from the same domain., providing practical knowledge applicable to cybersecurity, network security, and digital defense.

cards.title: CORS Proposed as a Solution (2009)
cards.description: The World Wide Web Consortium (W3C) introduced Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) as a structured way to allow safe cross-origin requests. cards.title: Preflight Requests in CORS (2011)
cards.description: To prevent unauthorized cross-origin requests, browsers introduced 'preflight' requests that check permissions before sending sensitive data. This card covers key aspects including For methods like `POST`, `PUT`, or `DELETE`, the browser first sends an `OPTIONS` request., The server must respond with the correct `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` and `Access-Control-Allow-Headers`., This prevents unauthorized modifications to data by checking if the server explicitly allows it., providing practical knowledge applicable to cybersecurity, network security, and digital defense.
